experiment: 3-tier funnel: binary scan -> top-C1 by Hamming -> PQ-ADC re-rank (RAM) -> top-C2 -> exact rerank C2. Cohere 1M x 1024. Metric: recall@10 + exact reads (=C2, = SSD reads in disk regime) vs 2-tier baseline (binary->exact, C1 reads). src/bin/funnel3.rs. Task #1 (experiment B).
note: B WORKS - if the PQ prune tier is accurate enough. 2-tier C1=1000 = 0.999 @ 1000 exact reads. 3-tier with M=64 PQ (64 B/vec in RAM): 0.989 recall at C2=128 = 7.8x FEWER reads, 0.955 at C2=64 = 15.6x fewer. M=32: 0.920 @ C2=128. M=16: too coarse (0.732 @ C2=128). So a 64 B/vec PQ prune tier (RAM, cache-resident) cuts the EXACT/SSD reads ~8x at ~0.99 recall - directly fixing the carousel x disk death-spiral (052), where C=1000 random SSD reads/query was the killer; now ~128. Tradeoff: PQ accuracy (M) vs reads saved. Only matters in the disk regime (RAM rerank is already cheap, 015/037).
